stories.headline: Denver braces for hard freeze, spring snow as sharp cold front approaches
stories.summary:
The 9NEWS Weather Impact Team has issued a Weather Impact Alert for Friday and Saturday, warning Denver metro residents of a hard freeze Saturday morning with temperatures expected to drop into the low-to-upper 20s — a dramatic swing from mid-70s warmth forecast for Thursday afternoon. Light snow accumulation is possible in Denver by Friday night, which would be the city's first measurable snowfall since March 15.

Before the cold arrives, Red Flag Warnings are in effect south and east of Denver on Thursday, with wind gusts of 35 to 45 mph and critically low relative humidity creating dangerous fire conditions. A sharp cold front will push through Colorado early Friday morning, sending temperatures crashing into the 40s by Friday afternoon with a rain-snow mix developing.

Gardeners are being urged to hold off on planting as the hard freeze could devastate early spring vegetation. Black ice is also a concern for Friday night and Saturday morning commuters. Denver's average last freeze date falls in early May, and freezes have been recorded as late as Memorial Day in some years.

stories.headline: Colorado awards $26 million in tax incentives to unnamed aerospace firm that could bring 1,250 jobs
stories.summary:
The Colorado Economic Development Commission approved $26 million in job growth incentive tax credits Thursday for an unnamed aerospace and defense company referred to as 'Project Hera,' the largest such incentive package awarded by the state so far in 2026. The company, which researches, designs and manufactures cutting-edge technology systems, is weighing expansion sites in Douglas or Jefferson counties against competing offers from Alabama, Florida and Pennsylvania.

If the company chooses Colorado, the expansion is projected to add more than 1,000 jobs with an average annual salary of $122,000 within four years. State officials said Douglas County has already committed significant additional incentives to attract the project, and the company is seeking to expand its existing federal contracts while pursuing new ones.

The approval comes as revised federal data shows Colorado lost approximately 11,700 jobs in 2025, and amid a Colorado Chamber Foundation report that the state suffered a net loss of 34 public company headquarters since 2022. The incentive package is the latest in a string of aerospace-focused awards from the EDC, which approved a $35 million package for another defense firm last December.

stories.headline: Teacher sues Denver police, Auraria campus over 2024 pro-Palestinian protest arrest
stories.summary:
A Denver teacher has filed a federal civil rights lawsuit against Denver police and Auraria campus officials stemming from her arrest during a pro-Palestinian demonstration on the Auraria campus in April 2024. Margaret Gutberlet, represented by Denver firm Newman McNulty, alleges officers violated her rights to free speech and assembly when they arrested her on suspicion of trespassing and failing to follow a lawful order after she joined protesters sitting on the campus quad.

The complaint, filed Tuesday in U.S. District Court, alleges that Denver police and Auraria officials targeted anti-war protesters specifically and conducted a mass arrest without probable cause, without sufficient body-camera documentation, and without assessing whether individuals had actually violated any law. Gutberlet was held in jail for more than 12 hours before being released; the charges against her were later dropped.

The lawsuit also claims Denver police failed to follow recommendations from the city's police watchdog issued after the 2020 George Floyd protests, establishing a pattern of civil rights violations against demonstrators. The complaint alleges 10 violations of state and federal law, including unlawful arrest, malicious prosecution and conspiracy, and seeks an unspecified amount in damages. The Auraria Campus said it had not been served with the lawsuit and had no comment on its substance.

stories.headline: Denver restaurant owners push back against proposal to cut tipped minimum wage
stories.summary:
More than 65 Denver restaurant owners have signed on in opposition to any reduction of Denver's tipped minimum wage, currently set at $16.27 per hour, challenging the findings of a city-commissioned report that sparked a legislative push to lower the rate. The February report, titled 'The State of Denver Restaurants' and commissioned by Denver Economic Development and Opportunity, Visit Denver, and private restaurant financing company inKind, cited the tipped wage as a significant industry burden.

The Denver Auditor's Office released its own analysis this month identifying three specific claims in the report as inaccurate, including the use of outdated Bureau of Labor Statistics data to portray full-service restaurant closures as more severe than they actually were. Updated BLS data shows Denver had 910 full-service restaurants in the first quarter of 2023 and 795 by the fourth quarter of 2024 — a decline, but not the dramatic collapse depicted in the original report.

The debate became possible after House Bill 25-1208, signed by Gov. Jared Polis in June 2025 and effective January 1, 2026, allowed cities to adjust their own tip credit for the first time. Denver's overall minimum wage stands at $19.29 per hour in 2026, a 74 percent increase since 2019. City Council member Darrell Watson, who had indicated he was drafting legislation to lower the tipped wage, has not introduced any bill, and his office said no such measure is currently before the council.

stories.headline: Colorado lawmakers launch deep review of Medicaid program amid repeated payment errors and looming cuts
stories.summary:
Colorado's bipartisan Joint Budget Committee has set aside $500,000 to establish a working group for a comprehensive review of the state's Medicaid program, which serves more than 1.1 million residents and carries an $18 billion annual budget. Lawmakers have grown increasingly frustrated after discovering a series of multimillion-dollar payment errors within the program while simultaneously being forced to make painful cuts to services for immigrant children and people with disabilities.

The frustration reached a breaking point last month when nearly every state senator signed on to a draft resolution calling for the removal of HCPF executive director Kim Bimestefer, who resigned shortly after Gov. Jared Polis' office was informed of the resolution. The working group is expected to meet over the summer and fall, produce a report, and deliver recommendations for the incoming governor, who will take office early next year after Polis reaches his term limit.

Colorado Medicaid costs have doubled over the past decade, with the program placing a growing strain on the state's general fund. Federal cuts and new work requirements included in legislation signed by President Donald Trump last summer are expected to add further cost pressure. Sen. Judy Amabile, a Boulder Democrat and Joint Budget Committee member, warned colleagues that this year's difficult cuts may only be the beginning.

stories.headline: Front Range Passenger Rail plan draws community support — and skepticism — in Boulder
stories.summary:
About 150 Boulder residents attended a community meeting Wednesday night to hear details of the Front Range Passenger Rail District's $331 million 'starter service' plan, which would link Denver and Boulder by train beginning in January 2029 with three daily roundtrips at speeds up to 79 mph. The route would also stop at Westminster, Broomfield, Louisville, Longmont, Loveland and Fort Collins, running on existing RTD B-Line tracks before shifting to Burlington Northern Santa Fe Railway freight lines.

The meeting was largely enthusiastic, though some attendees expressed disappointment that the starter service falls well short of the high-frequency, high-speed rail many Boulder residents have long envisioned. State officials are also rallying support for a ballot tax measure that would eventually fund an expanded Colorado Connector service with 10 daily roundtrips linking Fort Collins to Pueblo.

A lingering sore point at the meeting was RTD's failure to deliver the FasTracks Denver-Boulder rail line that voters approved and funded with a sales tax increase in 2004. Boulder County Commissioner Claire Levy, who sits on the rail district board, acknowledged that RTD has set aside approximately $190 million in a savings account from those revenues and is expected to contribute a share — estimated at roughly $25 million annually — toward the new service's operating costs. Multiple boards and RTD directors must still approve the funding before construction can proceed.

stories.headline: Broncos ownership group acquires largest minority stake in Colorado Rockies for $672 million
stories.summary:
The Penner Sports Group, the ownership entity behind the Denver Broncos, has acquired the largest minority ownership stake in the Colorado Rockies in a deal valued at $672 million, according to reports confirmed by multiple outlets. The transaction represents a significant cross-sport investment by the group led by Walmart heir Rob Walton, who purchased the Broncos in 2022.

The acquisition makes the Penner group the single largest minority stakeholder in the Rockies franchise, though the Monfort family retains controlling ownership of the MLB club. Financial details of the ownership structure were not immediately disclosed.

The deal deepens the ties between two of Denver's major professional sports franchises and signals continued high-dollar investor interest in Colorado sports properties despite the Rockies' recent on-field struggles.

stories.headline: Avalanche set for first-round Stanley Cup Playoff series against Los Angeles Kings
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche will face the Los Angeles Kings in the first round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs, according to Colorado Hockey Now. The matchup pits two of the Western Conference's top teams against each other in what is expected to be a competitive opening-round series.

The Avalanche closed out the regular season with a pair of results reported earlier this week and enter the playoffs as a contending team behind star center Nathan MacKinnon, who reached the 400-goal milestone in the regular-season finale. The team also recalled defenseman Joe Ahcan from AHL Colorado ahead of the playoff roster being set.

No dates or times for the series opener have been confirmed in local reporting. Colorado's playoff run will be closely watched after the team's Stanley Cup championship in 2022 and a string of deep postseason runs in recent years.

stories.headline: Denver Nuggets to play regular-season game in Mexico City against Indiana Pacers in November
stories.summary:
The NBA has announced that the Denver Nuggets will face the Indiana Pacers in a regular-season game in Mexico City on November 7, 2026. It will be the Nuggets' second game played in Mexico, continuing the league's international expansion efforts.

Three-time NBA MVP Nikola Jokic is expected to headline the Nuggets' roster for the game, alongside All-Star guard Jamal Murray and forward Aaron Gordon. The Nuggets are preparing for their first-round playoff series against the Minnesota Timberwolves before the November international game.

The Mexico City matchup is part of the NBA's broader strategy to grow the game in Latin America, where the league has developed a significant fan base over the past two decades.

stories.headline: Front Range Passenger Rail's 'Colorado Connector' to add Boulder County stops starting in 2029
stories.summary:
The Front Range Passenger Rail District confirmed this week that the Colorado Connector — the newly named passenger rail service selected through a statewide naming campaign — will join the Union Station schedule in 2029 as part of the broader Front Range Passenger Rail project. The service will include stops across Boulder County as part of its initial starter service linking communities from Fort Collins to the Denver metro.

The name was chosen following a statewide public awareness campaign that culminated in a public vote. Officials say the branding effort is designed to build community identity and public support for the project ahead of a potential ballot measure that would fund expanded service.

The rail district is presenting the project at a series of community meetings across the Front Range to build momentum and solicit feedback before funding decisions are finalized.

stories.headline: El Paso County's Paint Mines open for visitor season with new interpretive staff, trail counters
stories.summary:
El Paso County Parks Department is gearing up for the upcoming visitor season at the Paint Mines Interpretive Park in Calhan, a geologically and historically significant site on Colorado's eastern plains. The park has hired new interpretive staff who began working weekends — Fridays through Sundays — this week, guiding visitors through an area where humans have been documented for approximately 12,000 years.

Park staff are also deploying trail counters this season to collect visitor data for the first time, with the goal of better understanding usage patterns and improving community services. Officials say the data will help the department make decisions about staffing, infrastructure and programming in coming years.

The Paint Mines, characterized by colorful spires and formations carved by erosion, draw visitors from across the region. Colorado is also marking its 150th anniversary as a state this year, with parks officials highlighting the site as a long-cherished destination in the state's landscape.

joke.joke: Homeschool is when the teacher calls in sick, the substitute is also your mom, and you can't skip because the bus doesn't come.

weekendEvents.title: USWNT vs. Japan
weekendEvents.description: The U.S. Women's National Team closes out its April series against Japan in one of women's soccer's most iconic rivalries. The USWNT has consistently delivered dominant performances at this venue.
weekendEvents.location: DICK's Sporting Goods Park, Commerce City

weekendEvents.title: Colorado Rockies vs. Los Angeles Dodgers
weekendEvents.description: The Colorado Rockies open a home series against the Los Angeles Dodgers. Cold weather is expected, so dress accordingly.

weekendEvents.title: Colorado Nuggets vs. Minnesota Timberwolves — NBA Playoffs Game 1
weekendEvents.description: The Denver Nuggets open Round 1 of the NBA Playoffs against the Minnesota Timberwolves at Ball Arena.

weekendEvents.title: Colorado Rapids 30th Anniversary Match vs. Inter Miami CF (feat. Lionel Messi)
weekendEvents.description: The Colorado Rapids mark 30 years since their inaugural home match by hosting Inter Miami CF at Empower Field at Mile High. Lionel Messi is scheduled to appear.
weekendEvents.location: Empower Field at Mile High, Denver

weekendEvents.title: 2026 Denver Ramen Festival Presented by Fortune
weekendEvents.description: Denver's biggest noodle festival returns with two sessions featuring unlimited Asian bites, drinks, live music, cultural performances, and a JDM Car Show. Session one general admission begins at 1:00 PM; session two general admission begins at 5:30 PM. Ages 21+ only.

weekendEvents.title: Elitch Gardens Opening Weekend
weekendEvents.description: Denver's major theme park opens for the 2026 season with roller coasters and attractions. Water slides may not yet be in full swing given spring temperatures.

weekendEvents.title: 88 Drive-In Theatre — 50th Anniversary Season Opening
weekendEvents.description: The last drive-in movie theater in the Denver metro celebrates its 50th anniversary season, screening 'The Super Mario Galaxy Movie' and 'Hail Mary.' Outside food is now permitted with a $10 permit. Box office opens at 6:30 PM.

weekendEvents.title: Sustainable Development in Africa Conference
weekendEvents.description: The Africa Center and Students for Africa at the University of Denver's Josef Korbel School host this free conference bringing academics and cultural leaders together around the theme 'Reimagining Africa's Future Through Democracy, Economic Growth, and Youth Innovation.'
weekendEvents.location: Sie Complex, Room 5025, 2201 South University, University of Denver

weekendEvents.title: Black on Purpose: Joint Community Organization Discussion & Candidates Forum
weekendEvents.description: Community leaders, elected officials, and organizations from across Colorado convene for discussions on faith, business, education, civic leadership, and grassroots organizing under the theme 'Now & Next for Black Citizens in Colorado: Vision, Voice & a Viable Plan of Action.' Includes candidates' forums and panels.
weekendEvents.location: Clayton College Early Learning Administration Building, 3801 East MLK Jr. Boulevard, Denver

weekendEvents.title: Colorado Writers Showcase — Stories on Stage 25th Season
weekendEvents.description: Celebrating its 25th season, Stories on Stage presents renowned actors performing short stories by local authors Laura Pritchett, Cynthia Swanson, and Erika Krouse. A talk-back with performers follows the show.
weekendEvents.location: Su Teatro Cultural and Performing Arts Center, 721 Santa Fe Drive, Denver

weekendEvents.title: Party for Our Planet — Children's Museum of Denver
weekendEvents.description: The Children's Museum of Denver hosts an Earth Day celebration with activities focused on the natural world, geared toward families and young children.

weekendEvents.title: Record Store Day
weekendEvents.description: The annual international celebration of independent record stores returns, with special vinyl releases and deals at participating shops throughout the Denver metro area.
weekendEvents.location: Independent record stores throughout Denver metro

topStory.headline: Democrat Mejia Wins New Jersey Special Election, Narrowing House Republican Majority
topStory.summary:
Democrat Analilia Mejia won Thursday's special election in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District, defeating Republican Joe Hathaway and retaining the seat for Democrats. The Associated Press called the race for Mejia minutes after polls closed, and she addressed supporters in Montclair, describing herself as an "unbought, unbossed, sassy new member of Congress."

Mejia, 48, is a former head of the Working Families Alliance and onetime staffer for Sen. Bernie Sanders, who backed her campaign along with Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez. She ran on a progressive platform that included abolishing Immigration and Customs Enforcement, raising taxes on the wealthy, and establishing universal health care and child care. She also positioned herself as a direct opponent of President Donald Trump, criticizing his pardons of Jan. 6 defendants and his administration's freezing of congressionally authorized funds.

The seat was vacated when Democrat Mikie Sherrill left Congress to become New Jersey's governor. Mejia emerged from a competitive February primary — winning with less than 30 percent of the vote — over a field that included former Rep. Tom Malinowski, former Lt. Gov. Tahesha Way, and Essex County Commissioner Brendan Gill. She outraised Republican Hathaway, a Randolph Township Council member, by roughly two to one, collecting just over $1 million through the end of March, more than half from small-dollar donors.

Mejia's victory narrows the Republican House majority to 218-214 and adds to a string of Democratic wins in special elections since Trump returned to office. The 11th District, which then-Vice President Kamala Harris carried by 9 points in 2024 and Sherrill won by 15 points in her gubernatorial race, was considered a favorable environment for Democrats. Mejia will serve the remainder of Sherrill's term through January; a primary is scheduled for June to determine candidates for a full term beginning in 2027.
